## 1. Chat Pages/Routes

There are **no dedicated chat page routes**. Chat functionality is embedded as a **global Modal + Floating Widget** within the shared `AppLayout` (at `/data/project/game-workspace/apps/web/src/layouts/AppLayout.tsx`), and also used locally in several pages.

### Global Chat (AppLayout) -- available everywhere
- **`FloatingChatWidget`** -- draggable floating bubble at bottom-right, shown on every authenticated page (AppLayout, line 651). Shows total unread badge count, click opens a Popover with conversation snippets.
- **`ConversationList`** -- used inside the notification bell Popover in the AppLayout header (AppLayout, lines 477-485). Displays the full conversation list split into "unread" and "read" sections.
- **`ChatModal`** -- a full-screen-style modal opened from the notification bell or FloatingChatWidget, bound via `globalChatPartner` state (AppLayout, line 648).

### CS Dispatch Page -- primary CS-facing chat usage
- **`/data/project/game-workspace/apps/web/src/pages/dispatch/CSDispatchView.tsx`** -- the main CS dispatch workbench at route `/cs/dispatch`. Shows a sidebar of companions (line 192-365). Clicking a companion opens a `ChatModal` (line 652). This is the primary interface where CS communicates with companions.

## 1. WebSocket Gateway (`/data/project/game-workspace/apps/server/src/ws/ws.gateway.ts`)

### File Path
`/data/project/game-workspace/apps/server/src/ws/ws.gateway.ts` (463 lines)

### Module Registration
`/data/project/game-workspace/apps/server/src/ws/ws.module.ts` -- exports `WsGateway` so other modules (chat, orders, billing, companions, agent) can inject it.

### Room Management (Connection: lines 78-127)

On `handleConnection` (line 78), every authenticated socket joins **4 room patterns**:

| Room Pattern | Example | Who Joins | Line |
|---|---|---|---|
| `user:{userId}` | `user:abc123` | All users | 97 |
| `studio:{studioId}` | `studio:studio1` | All users with a studioId | 100 |
| `companion:{companionId}` | `companion:comp1` | Companion users only | 103 |
| `pc:{companionId}` | `pc:comp1` | Companion users only | 104 |

Additionally, the gateway maintains two in-memory maps:
- `companionSockets: Map<companionId, socketId>` (line 48)
- `userSockets: Map<userId, socketId>` (line 50)

On connection for companions (lines 102-122):
- Companion status set to `AVAILABLE` in DB (line 108-111)
- Attendance record created via `companionsService.ensureAttendance()` (line 114)
- `status:broadcast` event emitted to entire studio room (line 117)
